title: Inconsistent error with podman build using --secret
body:
### Issue Description

Attempting to build an image using `podman build` along with a secret using `--secret` will inconsistently return the following error:
```
time="2023-03-23T09:59:08-04:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* archive/tar: write too long\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Containerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testimage&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&secrets=%5B%22id%3Dmysecret%2Csrc%3Dpodman-build-secret3109220150%22%5D&shmsize=67108864&t=testimage": io: read/write on closed pipe
```
This only occurs some of the time. Running the exact command multiple times will occasionally build the image without any error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Containerfile:
```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:23.04

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret cat /run/secrets/mysecret
```

mysecret.txt:
```
secretpassword
```

Run:
```sh
$  podman build --secret id=mysecret,src=mysecret.txt -t testimage .
```

### Describe the results you received

The `podman build` command will occasionally return the following error:
```
time="2023-03-23T09:59:08-04:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* archive/tar: write too long\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v4.4.2/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Containerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testimage&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&secrets=%5B%22id%3Dmysecret%2Csrc%3Dpodman-build-secret3109220150%22%5D&shmsize=67108864&t=testimage": io: read/write on closed pipe
```

### Describe the results you expected

The same `podman build` command will alternatively run successfully:
```
STEP 1/2: FROM ubuntu:23.04
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret cat /run/secrets/mysecret
secretpassword
COMMIT testimage
--> Pushing cache []:319b70baa4bd06570e3c7fdf2f225fd3293ee98170ccb5272dce831c6bd34ee5
--> dc3a689b5c0
Successfully tagged localhost/testimage:latest
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

This is a Windows 10 installation of Podman installed using `winget install RedHat.Podman`.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Aetylus Is content in `secret.txt` too large ? or how big is your `contextdir` ?

> @Aetylus Is content in `secret.txt` too large ? or how big is your `contextdir` ?

It shouldn't be, as it's 15 characters long encoded in UTF-8, for a total of 16 bytes (carriage return included).

As far as the `contextdir` goes, do you mean the total size of all files in the build directory? If so, it's just the two files, the `Containerfile` and `secret.txt` for a total of 100 bytes even. 16 for `secret.txt` and 84 for `Containerfile`.

@Aetylus I tried it running in a loop for continuously for more than 20 times and error did not occur to me, it seems issue could have been with the old version, latest upstream does not have this issue `4.6.0-dev`, I'd request you to update both `podman-remote` client and server to the latest released version. I'm closing this issue but please re-open if you think if this persists in the recent version as well.

FYI
I see the same issue (using `--secret...` triggering the issue) using podman on Windows. (podman version 4.6.0 and 4.6.1)
But when I use WSL (Ubuntu 22.04) there is no issue.
WSL method requires doing a podman build within the quay.io/podman image version 4.6.0. As I need at least 4.0.0 and Ubuntu has version 3.4.4 .
So I guess this is an issue with the Windows build of podman.

> I tried it running in a loop for continuously for more than 20 times and error did not occur to me, it seems issue could have been with the old version, latest upstream does not have this issue `4.6.0-dev`, I'd request you to update both `podman-remote` client and server to the latest released version. I'm closing this issue but please re-open if you think if this persists in the recent version as well.
> \- @flouthoc

I tested on three systems:

`Windows 11 Pro (22621.2428) podman 4.7.1 + podman machine in WSL2`
`Windows 11 Enterprise (22621.2428) podman 4.6.2 + podman machine in WSL2`
These two machines will fail the first build, and the rest will succeed. I tried a few minutes later, and it still succeeds. But trying an hour or two later (I wasn't timing it) again triggers 1 failure, and further retries succeed.

`Windows Server 2022 Datacenter Azure Edition (20348.2031) podman 4.7.1 + podman machine in WSL2`
Always fails, no matter how often it's run.

I tested it with the `Steps to reproduce the issue` from above. It looks like it's system dependent, but I'm not sure what differences to looks for.
